nativeRange.habitat: Originally collected from the Vietnamese province of Thanh Hoa, where it occurs in stagnant and slow-flowing waters typical of South-East Asian wetlands and rice-paddy margins.

decorPreferences: soft to moderately hard water tolerated
iron-rich root tabs and micronutrient dosing
open midground space around rosette for arching leaves

summary: Vietnamese Eriocaulon (Eriocaulon cinereum) is a grass-like rosette plant from Thanh Hoa, Vietnam, widely regarded as one of the more accessible members of the genus. It forms a bushy midground cluster of arching, needle-like, medium-green leaves and tolerates harder water than most other Eriocaulons.
careNotes: Eriocaulon Vietnam is the trade name for the cultivated form of Eriocaulon cinereum collected from the Vietnamese province of Thanh Hoa. It is sold under several names in the hobby, including 'Eriocaulon sp. Vietnam', 'Eriocaulon cinereum Vietnam', and 'Vietnamese Pipewort'. Among Eriocaulons available to aquarists — a genus that includes notoriously demanding species such as E. setaceum, E. ratnagiricum, and E. quinquangulare — the Vietnam form has earned a reputation for noticeably easier care. It is widely reported to tolerate moderately hard water, making it one of the few Eriocaulons accessible to keepers who do not use RO or DI water, though soft, acidic conditions still tend to produce the best growth. The plant forms a bushy rosette of long, arching, medium-green needle-like leaves and is typically placed in the midground, or in the background of smaller nano aquariums, where its form can bridge low foreground groundcovers and taller stem plants. Unlike many rosette plants, it does not produce runners, which simplifies placement and makes repositioning straightforward. A nutrient-rich aquasoil substrate — such as ADA Amazonia, Tropica Soil, or UNS Controsoil — with iron-rich root tabs is strongly recommended, alongside balanced macro (NO3, PO4, K) and micro (especially iron) column dosing. Tissue-cultured plants generally transition to submerged growth more reliably than emersed-grown nursery stock. Like the rest of the genus, Eriocaulon Vietnam tends to be sensitive to sudden parameter swings and substrate disturbance, so established tanks with stable water chemistry are preferable.
breedingNotes: Eriocaulon Vietnam is propagated primarily by division. Once the mother rosette develops lateral shoots at its base, it can be gently lifted and separated into several daughter plants, each with its own root system, and replanted individually. The plant occasionally develops submerged inflorescences bearing adventitious plantlets, which can be detached and grown on once they have developed their own roots. Stem cuttings are possible but this species does not respond well to frequent trimming; cuttings should be kept to around 5 cm or less, and keepers who trim regularly tend to find it more practical to replace trimmed plants rather than rely on cuttings for propagation.

primary.name: Buce Plant (Eriocaulon sp Vietnam)
primary.notes: Primary data source for genus/family (Eriocaulon, Eriocaulaceae), common name (Vietnamese Eriocaulon), origin (Vietnam), light (high), CO2 (high, injection required), pH (4.5-7.0), water type (soft preferred), substrate (acidic required), growth rate (medium), difficulty (difficult), placement (midground or background), propagation (cuttings, max ~5 cm, does not respond well to trimming), and fertilization (NO3, PO4, iron, micronutrients).

additional.name: Aquasabi (Eriocaulon sp. 'Vietnam')
additional.notes: Cross-checked size (15-30 cm tall, medium-green arching leaves), origin (Thanh Hoa, Vietnam), lighting (medium sufficient, higher promotes compact growth), CO2 (at least 20 mg/L), hardness (tolerates hard water, soft preferred), placement (midground/background, Dutch-style scapes), growth rate (medium), difficulty (notably easier than other Eriocaulons - 'has already proved to be an easy aquarium plant'), propagation (division and adventitious plantlets), and trait that it does not develop runners.

additional.name: Hygronature - Care Guide: Eriocaulon Vietnam
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22-28 degC), pH (6.0-7.0), lighting (moderate to high), placement (foreground/midground), propagation (division), and fertilization (liquid fertilizer 1-2x weekly). Used as third source to triangulate temperature and pH ranges where Buce Plant gave a wider pH (4.5-7.0) and Aquasabi did not specify temperature.

additional.name: 2HrAquarist - How to grow Eriocaulon vietnam
additional.notes: Returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch but indexed via Google search snippet, which corroborated 60+ umol PAR lighting recommendation, soft-water + CO2-injected preference, and that the plant can grow under medium lighting but higher intensity promotes compact growth. Listed as supporting reference; not a successfully fetched page.

propagation: short stem cuttings (~5 cm; does not respond well to frequent trimming)
